mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate transmits easier financial conditions to equities by lowering discount rates and supporting risk-taking, so a downtrend in FEDFUNDS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in a majority of periods with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the sign of the relationship flips for more than one 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in retail sales signals weakening household demand and revenue pressure for large-cap firms, so RSAFS declines below its recent trend predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls below its 3-month moving average by more than 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered declines in RSAFS are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances, or if the negative return edge disappears in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate indicates weakening labor income and demand, which compresses earnings expectations and raises recession odds; when UNRATE rises quickly, S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days are negative.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment spikes are not followed by negative 60-day forward returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the relationship is not statistically distinct from zero in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in industrial production reflects stronger real activity and operating leverage for firms, so a sustained rise in INDPRO predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 6-month average by at least 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if above-trend INDPRO periods do not produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more often than not, or if the conditional average return is non-positive over any 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts surge captures an easing credit and demand impulse that spills into construction, materials, and consumption, so HOUST strength predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 6-month moving average by more than 5%
failure_condition: Reject if housing-starts breakouts are followed by negative or flat 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of cases, or if the edge vanishes after one year of walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of payroll growth and falling unemployment indicates broad labor-market tightening and income support, which should lift equity returns over the next 20 trading days; the signal is strongest only when both series improve together.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE does not outperform either proxy alone, or if the combined signal fails to produce positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, elevated policy rates increase discount rates and pressure equity multiples; when FEDFUNDS is high and CPI inflation remains elevated, S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days should be negative.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 2.0% and CPIAUCSL is rising on a 6-month basis
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-rate/high-inflation regime does not precede negative 60-day returns in a majority of occurrences, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: During a recessionary demand shock, weak GDP growth together with soft labor and housing data signals earnings downgrades and de-risking, so the combined pattern predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if low-GDP, high-unemployment, weak-housing episodes are not followed by negative 20-day returns more often than baseline, or if the interaction loses sign consistency across two consecutive rolling years.
